pattern: Infection spreads along edges in both directions, so rebuilding the tree as an undirected graph and measuring the farthest node from the start gives the total minutes.
hints: Infection can travel from a node to its children and to its parent, so the rigid downward shape of a tree is not enough.
Turn the tree into an undirected adjacency map where each node links to its children and its parent.
From the start value, run a breadth-first or depth-first traversal over that graph and return the maximum distance reached, which equals the last minute any node is infected.

edge_inputs.description: asymmetric tree, start in the interior
start at the root spreads only downward
start at a leaf must climb up then down the far side

stress.description: random tree with a random infection origin among its values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    vals = list(range(1, n + 1))
    rng.shuffle(vals)
    start = rng.choice(vals)
    return [vals, start]
